generatedSentence: In a sophisticated workplace, a janitor known as a bit of a knave, yet surprisingly kinder, secretly enjoyed his education in deciphering alien objects. His true objective was hidden behind a facade of trendy knitwear, holding the secret to a powerful kyanite energy source, understanding its true value like a forgotten vowel in an ancient language. An emergency struck as a self-important highness—a true jackass—attempted to skyjack a transport of experimental trijets, threatening to enact an era of entropy and oblivion. The janitor, remembering his jujitsu tryouts, observed the unfolding harrow, realizing his past self had been jailed by inaction, while a simple oilcan and stale oatcakes were all he had. Amidst the draconian regime of the warden, the darkened interior of a colossal dreadnought felt like a prison. The janitor, feeling a deep unease and knowing the downside of failure, lucked out by hacking into the ship's systems. His fingers, as if afflicted by leprosy after long hours on a lathe, still moved with precision, though sweat dripped as a heckler's voice echoed, hedging bets on whether liberty was truly lost. With a decisive knockout punch to the system, the stalemate broke. The dreadnought crash-landed on a sandy shore, its systems now forming a workable, unitary system. The janitor, victorious, felt a surge of unity, a vector towards a nascent utopia, willfully choosing to vitalize the shattered remnants. The scent of jasmine filled the air, a symbolic winning hand dealt by fate, as if foretold by a tarot card. He was no longer underwater in despair.
panelSentences: In a sophisticated workplace, a janitor known as a bit of a knave, yet surprisingly kinder, secretly enjoyed his education in deciphering alien objects. His true objective was hidden behind a facade of trendy knitwear, holding the secret to a powerful kyanite energy source, understanding its true value like a forgotten vowel in an ancient language.

An emergency struck as a self-important highness—a true jackass—attempted to skyjack a transport of experimental trijets, threatening to enact an era of entropy and oblivion. The janitor, remembering his jujitsu tryouts, observed the unfolding harrow, realizing his past self had been jailed by inaction, while a simple oilcan and stale oatcakes were all he had.

Amidst the draconian regime of the warden, the darkened interior of a colossal dreadnought felt like a prison. The janitor, feeling a deep unease and knowing the downside of failure, lucked out by hacking into the ship's systems. His fingers, as if afflicted by leprosy after long hours on a lathe, still moved with precision, though sweat dripped as a heckler's voice echoed, hedging bets on whether liberty was truly lost.

With a decisive knockout punch to the system, the stalemate broke. The dreadnought crash-landed on a sandy shore, its systems now forming a workable, unitary system. The janitor, victorious, felt a surge of unity, a vector towards a nascent utopia, willfully choosing to vitalize the shattered remnants. The scent of jasmine filled the air, a symbolic winning hand dealt by fate, as if foretold by a tarot card. He was no longer underwater in despair.
rawPanelSentences: In a **sophisticated workplace**, a **janitor** known as a bit of a **knave**, yet surprisingly **kinder**, secretly **enjoyed** his **education** in deciphering alien **objects**. His true **objective** was hidden behind a facade of **trendy knitwear**, holding the **secret** to a powerful **kyanite** energy source, understanding its true **value** like a forgotten **vowel** in an ancient language.

An **emergency** struck as a self-important **highness**—a true **jackass**—attempted to **skyjack** a **transport** of experimental **trijets**, threatening to **enact** an era of **entropy** and **oblivion**. The janitor, remembering his **jujitsu** **tryouts**, observed the unfolding **harrow**, realizing his past self had been **jailed** by inaction, while a simple **oilcan** and stale **oatcakes** were all he had.

Amidst the **draconian** regime of the **warden**, the **darkened** interior of a colossal **dreadnought** felt like a prison. The janitor, feeling a deep **unease** and knowing the **downside** of failure, **lucked** out by **hacking** into the ship's systems. His fingers, as if afflicted by **leprosy** after long hours on a **lathe**, still moved with precision, though sweat **dripped** as a **heckler**'s voice echoed, **hedging** bets on whether **liberty** was truly **lost**.

With a decisive **knockout** punch to the system, the **stalemate** broke. The **dreadnought** crash-landed on a **sandy** shore, its systems now forming a **workable**, **unitary** system. The janitor, **victorious**, felt a surge of **unity**, a **vector** towards a nascent **utopia**, **willfully** choosing to **vitalize** the shattered remnants. The scent of **jasmine** filled the air, a symbolic **winning** hand dealt by fate, as if foretold by a **tarot** card. He was no longer **underwater** in despair.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, retro-futuristic animated style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a scruffy, unassuming man in a faded, patched jumpsuit, wielding a high-tech mop-staff, with glowing kyanite shards embedded in his utility belt. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The janitor, with a thoughtful expression, secretly examining a glowing kyanite crystal and intricate alien objects in a dimly lit, sophisticated high-tech server room, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A luxurious trijet transport is being skyjacked, with a flamboyant "highness" figure gloating over panicked passengers, while the janitor, hidden, watches from a ventilation shaft, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Inside the darkened, control room of a massive dreadnought, the janitor intensely hacks at a console, his face illuminated by screen glow, surrounded by menacing warden-like guards, close-up. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The dreadnought's wreckage on a serene, sandy beach, fragrant jasmine flowers blooming nearby, as the victorious janitor stands tall, overseeing a new, unified, and peaceful landscape, wide shot. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

Strictly thermodynamic entropy. A measure of the amount of energy in a physical system that cannot be used to do work.
A measure of the disorder present in a system.
definitions.example: Ludwig Boltzmann defined entropy as being directly proportional to the natural logarithm of the number of microstates yielding an equivalent thermodynamic macrostate (with the eponymous constant of proportionality). Assuming (by the fundamental postulate of statistical mechanics), that all microstates are equally probable, this means, on the one hand, that macrostates with higher entropy are more probable, and on the other hand, that for such macrostates, the quantity of information required to describe a particular one of its microstates will be higher. That is, the Shannon entropy of a macrostate would be directly proportional to the logarithm of the number of equivalent microstates (making it up). In other words, thermodynamic and informational entropies are rather compatible, which shouldn't be surprising since Claude Shannon derived the notation 'H' for information entropy from Boltzmann's H-theorem.

To water or irrigate insufficiently
definitions.example: Care must be taken not to underwater houseplants in the summer.

definitions.definition: Beneath the surface of the water, or of or pertaining to the region beneath the water surface
Beneath the water line of a vessel
Under water.
Having negative equity; owing more on an asset than its market value
definitions.example: We've been underwater on our mortgage ever since the housing crash.

definitions.definition: Going beneath the surface of the water
Trouble; misery; a feeling of disquiet or concern.
A unitary council.
A unitary matrix or operator.
Having the quality of oneness.
(of a system of government or administration) That concentrates power in a single body, rather than sharing it with more local bodies.
definitions.example: a unitary authority

definitions.definition: (of an algebra) That contains an identity element.
(of a matrix or operator) Whose inverse is equal to its adjoint.
A world in which everything and everyone works in perfect harmony.
The quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable.
definitions.example: The Shakespearean Shylock is of dubious value in the modern world.

definitions.definition: The degree of importance given to something.
definitions.example: The value of my children's happiness is second only to that of my wife.

definitions.definition: That which is valued or highly esteemed, such as one's morals, morality, or belief system.
definitions.example: He does not share his parents' values.

definitions.definition: The amount (of money or goods or services) that is considered to be a fair equivalent for something else.
The relative duration of a musical note.
definitions.example: The value of a crotchet is twice that of a quaver.

definitions.definition: The relative darkness or lightness of a color in (a specific area of) a painting etc.
Any definite numerical quantity or other mathematical object, determined by being measured, computed, or otherwise defined.
definitions.example: The exact value of pi cannot be represented in decimal notation.

definitions.definition: Precise meaning; import.
definitions.example: the value of a word; the value of a legal instrument

definitions.definition: (in the plural) The valuable ingredients to be obtained by treating a mass or compound; specifically, the precious metals contained in rock, gravel, etc.
definitions.example: The vein carries good values.

definitions.definition: Esteem; regard.
Valour; also spelled valew.
To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.
definitions.example: I will have the family jewels valued by a professional.

definitions.definition: To fix or determine the value of; assign a value to, as of jewelry or art work.
To regard highly; think much of; place importance upon.
definitions.example: Gold was valued highly among the Romans.

definitions.definition: To hold dear.
definitions.example: I value these old photographs.

definitions.definition: To give life to something; to animate.
To make more vigorous; to invigorate or stimulate.
A sound produced by the vocal cords with relatively little restriction of the oral cavity, forming the prominent sound of a syllable.
A letter representing the sound of vowel; in English, the vowels are a, e, i, o, u, and sometimes y.
To add vowel points to a consonantal script (e.g. niqqud in Hebrew or harakat in Arabic)
Being the winner in a contest, struggle, war, etc.
Of or expressing a sense of victory or triumph.
definitions.example: The team were met with a victorious cheer.

definitions.definition: A directed quantity, one with both magnitude and direction; the signed difference between two points.
An ordered tuple representing a directed quantity or the signed difference between two points.
Any member of a (generalized) vector space.
definitions.example: The vectors in {\mathbb Q}[X] are the single-variable polynomials with rational coefficients: one is \textstyle x^{42}+\frac1{137}x-1.

definitions.definition: A chosen course or direction for motion, as of an aircraft.
A carrier of a disease-causing agent.
(by extension) A person or entity that passes along an urban legend or other meme.
A recurring psychosocial issue that stimulates growth and development in the personality.
The way in which the eyes are drawn across the visual text. The trail that a book cover can encourage the eyes to follow from certain objects to others.

(operating systems) A memory address containing the address of a code entry point, usually one which is part of a table and often one that is dereferenced and jumped to during the execution of an interrupt.
A one-dimensional array.
A graphical representation using outlines; vector graphics.
definitions.example: a vector image

definitions.definition: A DNA molecule used to carry genetic information from one organism into another.
To set (particularly an aircraft) on a course toward a selected point.
To redirect to a vector, or code entry point.
